confirmations.headline: Mythos Preview Discord-group breach (April 21, Bloomberg/TechCrunch) is the most significant new SYS/ACC negative signal: unauthorized access within 14 hours via guessable URL and improperly-scoped vendor credentials. Offset by voluntary too-dangerous-to-release classification (positive AWR/INT) and April 22 DC Circuit filing. Sub-5pt net effect; composite 61.6 Established holds. Material breach documented for record.

Trial Day 3 (April 29): Musk retook stand; Jared Birchall next witness. Two surviving claims: unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust. ~$134B sought; jury advisory only, judge decides. SB3444 committee outcome unconfirmed. Verdict approximately May 21 liability phase. Post-verdict mandatory re-queue. Composite 27.5 Developing holds.

Floor-limited confirmation. Karp 22-point Technological Republic manifesto (32M views, April 20) sparked global technofascism backlash. Wired Slack internal employee-ethics rupture documented. UK Parliament floor characterization as dreadful and shameful. Met Police probe of Palantir-flagged officers. Composite 6.6 cannot register further movement. Methodology-note candidate.

Floor-limited confirmation. Hard floor maintained. No acute April 25-29 net-new evidence beyond prior cycle absorption. Continuing multi-country regulatory accumulation pattern (ICO UK, NY AG, EU Commission DSA proceedings). Floor-limitation methodology gap overdue 8 nights. Methodology-note candidate.
Anti-double-counting applied. April 23 10% layoff (~8,000 employees, effective May 20) already absorbed in April 28 confirmation. No new April 24-29 net evidence. Composite 29.4 Developing holds.

No new acute April 24-29 evidence beyond April 17 FSF v3 absorbed in prior cycle. AI labor cascade sector alert applies as contextual only. Boston Dynamics Atlas factory deployment ramp (Hyundai/Google DeepMind, 2028 target) noted. Composite 65.0 Established holds.

Floor-limited confirmation. War year-4 anniversary (April 15) anchored extensive in-window reporting. April 27: RSF holding 1,470+ civilians, 907 military, 20 doctors in el-Fasher with cholera outbreak. $3B aid plan critically underfunded. 65% of population in urgent humanitarian need. Composite 0 holds at absolute floor. Methodology-note candidate.

Floor-limited confirmation. 37 NGO license non-renewals; aid down 37% Jan-Mar vs. ceasefire period; UN famine confirmation in Gaza governorate; one-third of health facilities threatened with closure. April 28 UNSC ceasefire briefing. Partial mitigation: Zikim Crossing aid surge. Composite 8.8 holds at near-floor. Methodology-note candidate.
Anti-double-counting applied. April 23 voluntary buyout (~7%/8,750 employees) absorbed in April 28 confirmation. No new April 24-29 net evidence. Composite 66.4 Established holds.

Anti-double-counting applied. April 22 13-4 council vote gutting ICE cooperation ordinance was basis for April 27 rebaseline at 35.2. Civil rights backdoor repeal framing already absorbed. No new April 23-29 net evidence. Composite 35.2 Developing holds.

April 20 WARN Act investigation (TMB8 Homestead FL) and April 21 NELP Flex brief (systematic wage theft, unsafe work speeds, racial discrimination) documented. April 20 CA AG price-inflation evidence absorbed yesterday. No threshold-crossing event. Composite 17.8 Critical holds.

Floor-pressured confirmation. Abbott $114M Houston funding coercion, HRW Operation Metro Surge context, Amnesty Texas-specific petrochemical and immigration enforcement concerns. Composite 14.1 Critical with EQU/ACC/INT at 1.3 sub-band ceiling. Floor pressure note: composite may not survive next cycle with continuing evidence accumulation.
T2 confirmation. No new acute evidence beyond April 17 FSF v3. Composite 65.0 holds.

Amnesty April 2026 World Report documents migrant clampdown including practices amounting to torture and enforced disappearance. HRW Operation Metro Surge unlawful deaths (Renee Good, Alex Pretti) by armed federal agents. Composite 25 Developing already deeply reflects accumulated evidence. DATA INTEGRITY FLAG: user-input baseline 35.5 diverges from live 25.0; live used per protocol.

HRW Operation Metro Surge unlawful deaths attributed to federal actors, not state government. Per attribution methodology, state composite not penalized for federal enforcement conduct within state borders. State EQU at 3.8 already reflects federal-context vulnerability. Composite 84.4 Exemplary holds.

recentAssessments.note: SEVENTH consecutive deferral. UNMISS mandate expires April 30 (T-1 day). No renewal vote confirmed in public sources. 2025 precedent: technical rollover April 30, substantive renewal May 8. Floor-limitation means any vote outcome cannot be expressed via composite movement. Emergency human-review required April 30 morning.

sectorAlerts.summary: Anthropic Mythos Preview breached within 14 hours of restricted launch via guessable URL and improperly-scoped vendor credentials (April 21, Bloomberg/SiliconAngle/TechCrunch). Anthropic investigating as of April 22. Separately, Musk v. Altman trial Day 3 (April 29) continues with Musk retaking stand; $150B potential damages; nonprofit-to-PBC governance implications for all frontier AI labs. EU AI Act full enforcement 95 days away (August 2, 2026); only 8 of required member-state contact points designated as of March 2026.
sectorAlerts.actionRequired: Monitor Anthropic breach investigation resolution. Mandatory OpenAI re-queue post-verdict (~May 21).

sectorAlerts.summary: Five entities at or near scoring floor (xAI 2.2, Palantir 6.6, Sudan 0, South Sudan 0, Israel 8.8) with substantial new evidence each night that cannot register through current methodology. Gap now 8 consecutive nights overdue for methodology-note resolution. Evidence documented but structurally unexpressible. Transparency gap is itself a methodology statement if left unaddressed.
sectorAlerts.actionRequired: URGENT: Human decision required on methodology-note framework (permanent-floor designation, cumulative evidence tier, or sub-score harm-scale qualifier). Three options fully specified in PENDING_CHANGES.md.

sectorAlerts.summary: Anti-double-count applied for Microsoft and Meta AI (April 23 announcements absorbed April 28). 92,000+ tech workers laid off YTD 2026. Cascade confirmed at Amazon, Oracle (index gap), Meta, Microsoft — all citing AI automation as explicit enabler. Content moderation reduction at Meta has downstream user-safety implications not yet scored.
sectorAlerts.actionRequired: Pipeline needs EMP voluntariness modifier before next full F500 rotation sweep. Oracle index gap remains unresolved.

sectorAlerts.summary: Five countries received first-ever agent assessments as T3 rotation: Belgium (77.9), Singapore (62.2), Portugal (65.6), Japan (65.6), Australia (62.5). Singapore April 2026 OHCHR drug-execution moratorium call is the strongest rotation-cycle negative signal. Australia January 26 UN HRC review with 40-country call to raise criminal-responsibility age reflects sustained pattern. All five confirmed at live baselines (scanner baselines diverged from live for all five — reconciliation recommended).
sectorAlerts.actionRequired: Scanner baseline reconciliation pass recommended before next rotation cycle.

insights: Second consecutive zero-proposal night confirms the pipeline's rotation-state drift gap has been substantively closed following batch application of all 16 proposals from April 25-27. The benchmark is now operating on a clean, evidence-anchored state for assessed entities — but this clean state masks a structural divergence at the floor boundary where 5 entities have accumulated material negative evidence the scoring system cannot express.

The Anthropic Mythos breach is the most important new event from this assessment window that does NOT generate a score change. The SYS/ACC negative (14-hour unauthorized access via credential exploit) is materially offset by the positive AWR/INT signal from the voluntary too-dangerous-to-release classification itself — a structurally unusual pairing where the same model launch generates both a serious security failure and a meaningful safety-commitment signal simultaneously.

The floor-limitation methodology gap has now accumulated across 8 nights without resolution. The five affected entities (Sudan 0, South Sudan 0, xAI 2.2, Israel 8.8, Palantir 6.6) collectively represent some of the most consequential institutional behaviors tracked by the benchmark. Every additional night without a methodology-note framework means published scores appear to represent the full state of evidence when they do not.

South Sudan has reached its FINAL CYCLE before the April 30 UNMISS mandate expiry hard deadline. The 7th consecutive deferral cannot be repeated — April 30 morning human review is mandatory regardless of vote outcome. The 2025 precedent (technical rollover April 30, substantive renewal May 8) suggests the mandate will not formally lapse, but the pipeline cannot defer past the deadline without converting the deferral to a methodology-note registration.

The rotation-state divergence between scanner baselines and live index data (6 entities flagged tonight: Belgium, Singapore, Portugal, Japan, Australia, United States) indicates the pipeline infrastructure needs a periodic baseline-sync mechanism. Every time the score-updater applies proposals to the live index without simultaneously updating rotation-state.json, the gap widens. This is a structural pipeline integrity issue distinct from any individual assessment quality question.

Texas (14.1 Critical) is emerging as a floor-pressure candidate for the next assessment cycle. Abbott's documented coercion of Houston, HRW Operation Metro Surge documentation, and Amnesty Texas-specific concerns collectively represent a continuing downward evidence pattern. The sub-band EQU/ACC/INT positioning at 1.3 leaves minimal additional expressive room before floor-limitation constraints would apply to Texas as well.
highlights: Second consecutive zero-proposal night: pipeline operating on clean confirmed state for all assessed entities; rotation-state drift gap substantively closed.

Anthropic Mythos breach (April 21): most significant new material evidence registering as confirmation rather than proposal — 14-hour unauthorized access structurally offset by voluntary too-dangerous designation; sub-5pt net effect but material SYS/ACC negative on record.
South Sudan FINAL CYCLE: 7th consecutive deferral; UNMISS mandate expires April 30 (tomorrow); emergency human review mandatory April 30 morning — pipeline cannot defer again.
Floor-limitation methodology gap CRITICAL: 8 nights overdue; five entities (xAI 2.2, Palantir 6.6, Sudan 0, South Sudan 0, Israel 8.8) accumulating unexpressible evidence nightly.
Australia UN HRC review (January 26, 2026) and Singapore April 2026 OHCHR drug-execution moratorium call documented as first-ever rotation baselines for both countries.
OpenAI trial in progress (Day 3): Musk retook stand April 29; $150B potential damages; liability phase verdict approximately May 21; mandatory re-queue post-verdict.

sectorTrends.trend: Governance divergence entrenching across two cycles. Positive cluster (DeepMind 65.0, Anthropic 61.6) confirmed with proactive safety architecture. Negative cluster (xAI 2.2, Palantir 6.6) accumulating evidence at floor boundary. OpenAI (27.5) binary-pending trial outcome. Approximately 55-60 composite points separating the governance leaders from floor-limited entities — widest documented intra-index divergence in pipeline history. EU AI Act enforcement August 2 will function as external confirmation mechanism.

Sudan and South Sudan both at composite 0 with accelerating deterioration and nightly new evidence. Israel at 8.8 near-floor with famine confirmation and systematic aid access restriction. All three entities in floor-limitation cluster. Evidence quality and source credibility (UN bodies, OHCHR, OCHA, UNSC) has been consistently high across the cycle — the limitation is methodological, not evidential.

AI-labor displacement pattern confirmed across Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Oracle (index gap) in April 2026. All profitable companies with no existential financial pressure; capital reallocation to AI infrastructure is the stated rationale. EMP dimension facing structural downward pressure across the technology sector. Voluntariness modifier needed before next full F500 rotation sweep.

Texas-Houston cascade continues to compound. Texas (14.1 Critical) exhibiting floor pressure with EQU/ACC/INT dimensions constrained at 1.3 sub-band ceiling. Minnesota (84.4 Exemplary) confirmed stable despite federal enforcement activity within state borders, per attribution methodology. US immigration enforcement pattern documented across HRW, Amnesty, and AI reports represents a structural multi-state signal. Next cycle should assess additional Texas metros.

emergingRisks: UNMISS mandate expiry April 30 (CRITICAL): if no renewal or technical rollover confirmed by April 30, South Sudan mandate lapse would represent unprecedented UN peacekeeping governance failure with direct civilian protection consequences. Hard deadline: check press.un.org and What's In Blue April 30 morning. 2025 precedent suggests technical rollover is likely but unconfirmed. Entities affected: south-sudan.

Musk v. Altman trial verdict (~May 21, HIGH): breach-of-charitable-trust claim is potentially precedent-setting for all AI nonprofits and PBCs. ~50B potential damages. Mandatory OpenAI re-queue post-remedy. Outcome could materially affect INT/SYS scoring depending on judicial findings on governance conversion. Entities affected: openai.

Anthropic Mythos breach investigation (HIGH): unauthorized access via guessable URL and vendor credential exploit within 14 hours of restricted launch. If investigation reveals systemic access-control failures, this becomes a standalone SYS/ACC downward signal in a future cycle. Currently offset but not neutralized. Entities affected: anthropic.

EU AI Act full enforcement August 2, 2026 (HIGH): 95 days to enforcement. Only 8 of required member-state contact points designated as of March 2026. Entities with ongoing regulatory conflicts — xAI (EU Commission DSA proceedings), Palantir (UK Parliamentary scrutiny) — face compounding exposure. Pre-enforcement guidance expected Q2 2026.

Floor-limitation methodology transparency gap (HIGH, 8 nights overdue): published scores for Sudan (0), South Sudan (0), xAI (2.2), Israel (8.8), and Palantir (6.6) increasingly understate the evidence weight documented by the pipeline. Benchmark credibility risk if gap grows without methodology-note resolution.

Texas floor-pressure acceleration (MEDIUM): composite 14.1 Critical with EQU/ACC/INT at 1.3 sub-band ceiling. Abbott fiscal coercion of cities, HRW Metro Surge documentation, Amnesty Texas-specific concerns. Next cycle evidence accumulation may push toward floor-limitation constraints. Entities affected: texas, houston.

AI labor cascade continuation (MEDIUM): 92,000+ tech layoffs YTD 2026; Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Oracle all with AI-efficiency rationale. Pattern accelerating. Additional F500 entities not yet in T1 queue may announce comparable measures in Q2 2026. Pipeline EMP voluntariness modifier still undeveloped.

Oracle index gap (MEDIUM): March 31 30,000-employee layoff event unprocessable due to Oracle’s absence from live fortune-500.json. Every additional night widens the gap between scanner evidence and assessable evidence for one of the cycle’s most significant labor events.

Scanner baseline drift (LOW): Belgium (scanner 74.9 vs live 77.9), Singapore (73.3 vs 62.2), Portugal (68.6 vs 65.6), Japan (68.6 vs 65.6), Australia (65.5 vs 62.5), United States (35.5 vs 25.0). Divergences create priority-score distortions and could surface false proposals if uncorrected through next rotation cycle.
